verification.reason: The paper describes fruit pericarp as the tissue analyzed. RNA-seq used fruit at 'seven days after breaker.' Isotope feeding used '10 days post breaker.' ChIP used '3-4 days after breaker.' The E8 promoter is described as 'fruit-specific.' The extracted field stating '7-10 days post breaker' and 'fruit-specific E8 promoter' accurately captures the range of timepoints used across different experiments.

Figure 3b caption explicitly states: 'peel and flesh of WT fruit at 5 days after breaker.' The text states: 'Since SlMYB12 is not expressed in tomato flesh, flesh offered a robust control.' This confirms expression predominantly in peel, not flesh, at 5 days after breaker.
The paper confirms fruit-specific expression and phenotypes in tomato fruit. Fig. 5a states 'Pictures were taken at seven days after breaker' indicating ripe fruit stage.

The paper analyzes Del/Ros1 and Indigo tomato fruit. Fig. 5a shows pictures 'at seven days after breaker' (ripe fruit). Fig. 5c quantifies phenylpropanoids in fruit. The tissue and stage (tomato fruit, ripe) are supported.

The paper states fruit were 'harvested 10 days after breaker' for the resveratrol quantification (Fig. 6b legend). The 35S:VvStSy construct uses the CaMV 35S promoter as stated in the paper ('35S:VvStSy'). The description of 'Tomato fruit, 10 days post breaker, driven by CaMV 35S promoter' is supported.
Fig. 6 caption states 'Fruit were harvested 10 days after breaker' and the paper describes LjIFS expression driven by the fruit-specific E8 promoter.
Figure 6b,d captions state 'Fruit were harvested 10 days after breaker,' matching the extracted field.

ChIP experiments used fruit pericarp (Fig. 3a: 'Pericarp (peel plus flesh) samples... at 3 days after breaker'). qRT-PCR compared peel and flesh (Fig. 3b: 'peel and flesh of WT fruit at 5 days after breaker'). RNA-seq used fruit pericarp ('Fruit pericarp was ground into fine powder'). The description 'pericarp/peel' accurately reflects the tissues studied.

ChIP experiments used fruit pericarp (Fig. 3a: 'Pericarp (peel plus flesh) samples'). qRT-PCR compared peel and flesh of WT fruit (Fig. 3b: 'peel and flesh of WT fruit at 5 days after breaker'). These match 'Tomato fruit pericarp/peel'.
Experiments were conducted on tomato fruit pericarp (peel plus flesh) at 3 days after breaker (ChIP) and peel at 5 days after breaker (SlMYB12 ChIP), as described in Figs. 3a-c.
ChIP experiments used tomato fruit at 3-4 days after breaker; RNA-seq used fruit pericarp at 7 days after breaker. 'Tomato fruit pericarp' is consistent.
